 {"id":"bd-583u","title":"resolv: add host-probe coverage for gai_suspend timeout semantics on all-NULL request arrays","description":"Lift the observed host behavior for gai_suspend timeouts on all-NULL request arrays into repeatable probe/test coverage so degenerate async-DNS timeout semantics stay grounded in glibc rather than assumptions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:50:25.400965075Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:24:07.169628697Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:24:07.167438632Z","close_reason":"Added host-parity gai_suspend timeout probes for all-NULL request arrays using observed libc behavior.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-5ajp","title":"EPIC: Bugs found and fixed in comprehensive audit","description":"This epic tracks all bugs found and fixed during the comprehensive multi-round code audit. These are ALREADY FIXED — this epic serves as documentation and regression tracking.

TOTAL BUGS FIXED: 30+

CRITICAL FIXES (caused crashes):
- dl_iterate_phdr was a no-op stub → now delegates to host
- fopen/fdopen returned fake stream IDs → now delegate to host for real FILE*
- _IO_2_1_{stdin,stdout,stderr}_ shadowed host with zeros → removed exports
- setjmp/longjmp were abort stubs → now delegate to host
- on_exit handlers never called during exit() → now invoked in reverse order
- resolve_host_symbol_raw infinite recursion → fixed bootstrap ordering

HIGH FIXES (data corruption/incorrect behavior):
- iconv_abi apply_progress usize underflow → saturating_sub
- stdlib_abi gcvt/qgcvt negative ndigit → max(0) guard
- malloc_abi is_bump_ptr false positives → added magic sentinel check
- malloc_abi calloc reentrant overflow → checked_mul
- stdio_abi fprintf/vfprintf/setvbuf missing host delegation
- HashSlot missing #[repr(C)] → cast to Entry could read wrong fields
- pthread_cancel self-cancellation never triggers → calls pthread_testcancel

MEDIUM FIXES (errno/correctness):
- termios cfsetispeed/cfsetospeed missing errno
- signal_abi raise() missing errno
- wchar_abi mbtowc/wctomb missing EILSEQ errno
- unistd_abi read/write missing EFAULT errno
- fortify __getlogin_r_chk/__ttyname_r_chk ignoring nreal
- c11threads thrd_sleep using raw __errno_location
- poll_abi sched_yield missing errno
- nftw/fts_open using libc::lstat instead of raw syscall

LOW FIXES (code quality):
- resource_abi unnecessary Box allocation
- inet_abi hard-coded struct sizes → size_of/offset_of
- glibc_internal_abi redundant as isize as usize cast","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:28.813375546Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:28.813375546Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:28.813375546Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-5cv2p","title":"[MEDIUM] fuzz: differential errno-precedence harness for pathname/fd/flags syscalls","description":"[MEDIUM] fuzz: add differential errno-precedence harness for pathname/fd/flags syscalls

Add a structure-aware libFuzzer target in `crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/` that differentially compares FrankenLibC vs host libc errno/return-value precedence for wrappers with overlapping invalid-input axes.

Recommended syscall set:
- `renameat2` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:16151`)
- `signalfd4` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:16291`)
- `close_range` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:5043`)
- `eventfd_write` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:6184`)
- `fsconfig` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:16755`)

Fuzz shape:
- mutate fd values, flag bitmasks, null/non-null pointers, short strings, and mixed-invalid combinations
- differential oracle is host libc / syscall behavior on the same machine
- assert exact errno precedence, not just success vs failure

Why now:
- recent work has added many single-case parity tests here, but the surface is still combinatorial and easy to regress when precedence rules overlap.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:30:24.684465933Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:18:24.991059312Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","errno","fuzz","syscall"]}
+{"id": "bd-5cv2p", "title": "[MEDIUM] fuzz: differential errno-precedence harness for pathname/fd/flags syscalls", "description": "[MEDIUM] fuzz: add differential errno-precedence harness for pathname/fd/flags syscalls

Add a structure-aware libFuzzer target in `crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/` that differentially compares FrankenLibC vs host libc errno/return-value precedence for wrappers with overlapping invalid-input axes.

Recommended syscall set:
- `renameat2` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:16151`)
- `signalfd4` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:16291`)
- `close_range` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:5043`)
- `eventfd_write` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:6184`)
- `fsconfig` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:16755`)

Fuzz shape:
- mutate fd values, flag bitmasks, null/non-null pointers, short strings, and mixed-invalid combinations
- differential oracle is host libc / syscall behavior on the same machine
- assert exact errno precedence, not just success vs failure

Why now:
- recent work has added many single-case parity tests here, but the surface is still combinatorial and easy to regress when precedence rules overlap.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "ubuntu", "created_at": "2026-04-22T08:30:24.684465933Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-23T02:42:00.000000000Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["differential", "errno", "fuzz", "syscall"], "closed_at": "2026-04-23T02:42:00.000000000Z", "closed_by": "cc-libc", "notes": "Fuzz target fuzz_errno_precedence delivered (covers renameat2, signalfd4, close_range, eventfd_write, fsconfig with differential host-vs-ABI errno comparison). Build broken due to CString::as_ptr method-pointer issue; fixed in d4df1f5e (closure-wrap). Verified 251,275 fuzz runs in 31s, no crashes."}
 {"id":"bd-5fw","title":"Completion Program: Final release-exit contract + deterministic closure protocol","description":"Background:
- Current project state has strong partial coverage and many gates, but final completion is still fragmented across open epics and in-progress subsystem tasks.
- We need one deterministic closure contract so future execution and review do not rely on implied assumptions.

Goal:
- Define and enforce the exact end-state required to claim “finished, correct, and optimized” for the current declared replacement level.

Deliverables:
1) Explicit release-exit checklist for L0 (interpose) with objective pass/fail checks.
2) Staged upgrade requirements for L1/L2 progression (what must be true before claim changes).
3) Artifact bundle spec for every release candidate:
   - conformance reports,
   - reality report,
   - replacement/profile reports,
   - perf baselines + deltas,
   - evidence/log indices.
4) Gate ordering and ownership (which scripts/tests run in what order and why).

Acceptance Criteria:
- A clean checkout can run a single scripted release dry-run and produce a complete pass/fail dossier.
- No “manual interpretation” required to determine readiness.
- Any missing artifact or breached threshold fails deterministically with actionable diagnostics.

Test and Logging Requirements:
- Add unit tests for checklist parsing/validation logic.
- Add end-to-end release-dry-run script tests (strict and hardened where relevant).
- Emit structured run logs including trace_id, mode, gate_name, outcome, errno/exit_code, timing, and artifact paths.

Optimization/Proof Requirements (Extreme Optimization skill):
- Include benchmark baseline references for all release-critical hot paths.
- Require isomorphism proof references for any optimization included in the release window.

Porting/Spec-first Requirements (Porting-to-Rust skill):
- Reference behavior contracts from PLAN/EXISTING/PROPOSED/FEATURE_PARITY docs directly in acceptance checks so closure is specification-backed.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:34.154973750Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:18:04.920565120Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T18:18:04.920450105Z","close_reason":"EPIC complete: all 5 children closed. Release closure contract, gate DAG, dossier validator, proof binder, and branch diversity gate all delivered.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","release","roadmap","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1024,"issue_id":"bd-5fw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Planning update (Codex):

- Expanded `bd-2uro` with a self-contained North Star + non-negotiable contracts + phase map + proof-obligation index (so we do not need to consult PLAN_TO_PORT_GLIBC_TO_RUST.md for closure intent).
- Added new child `bd-5fw.4` (Proof Obligations Binder) and set `bd-5fw.3` to *block on* it. This is intended to make release-dossier requirements mechanical: theorem -> evidence -> gate mapping.
- Code audit note (from explorer): README’s healing-action list appears to mention `QuarantineStale`, but `frankenlibc-membrane::HealingAction` does not define it. Also, membrane currently refuses to compile without `runtime-math-production` feature. These should be treated as doc-claim drift and covered by `bd-3rw.5` doc drift gates.

Coordination:
- MCP Agent Mail is currently overloaded (`health_level=red`, as of 2026-02-12). Using bead comments for now.","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:49:34Z"},{"id":1025,"issue_id":"bd-5fw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ALL CHILDREN CLOSED: bd-5fw.1 (closure contract), bd-5fw.2 (gate DAG), bd-5fw.3 (dossier validator), bd-5fw.4 (proof binder), bd-5fw.5 (branch diversity gate). EPIC deliverables complete: release-exit checklist, gate ordering, artifact bundle spec, dossier validator. Closing EPIC. —PearlFinch","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:18:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5fw.1","title":"Closure contract schema v1: objective release invariants by replacement level","description":"Background:
- We need an explicit, machine-checkable definition of "finished" for L0/L1/L2/L3 so release claims are objective.

Scope:
- Define a canonical closure contract schema with versioning.
- Encode required invariants per level: symbol support claims, call-through limits, test gates, perf ceilings, artifact proofs.

Deliverables:
1) `closure_contract.v1` schema (human+machine readable).
2) Level-specific obligation matrix with pass/fail predicates.
3) Mapping from each obligation to a concrete command/artifact path.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Contract can be validated automatically.
- No requirement remains ambiguous or manual-only.
- Any unmet invariant yields deterministic failure reason.

Rationale:
- This creates a stable control plane for all downstream beads and prevents claim drift.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for schema validation and invariant rule parsing.
- E2E dry-run over intentionally broken contracts.
- Structured logs: trace_id, level, invariant_id, check_cmd, result, artifact_ref.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.361423630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:01:46.445449915Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T22:01:46.445431891Z","close_reason":"Implemented closure_contract.v1 schema + deterministic validator gate + broken-contract e2e tests + structured log enforcement","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["closure","governance","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.1","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1026,"issue_id":"bd-5fw.1","author":"Codex","text":"Starting bd-5fw.1. Plan: (1) define closure_contract.v1 schema and level obligations in docs, (2) implement machine validator + deterministic diagnostics, (3) add unit tests and broken-contract tests, (4) wire structured release-check logs.","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:53:10Z"},{"id":1027,"issue_id":"bd-5fw.1","author":"Codex","text":"Delivered closure contract schema + validator gate + integration tests.\n\nArtifacts:\n- tests/conformance/closure_contract.v1.json\n- scripts/check_closure_contract.sh\n- crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/closure_contract_test.rs\n- scripts/ci.sh (extended gates now include closure contract gate)\n\nVerification:\n- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test closure_contract_test -- --nocapture (6 passed)\n- scripts/check_closure_contract.sh (PASS)\n\nContract supports machine-validated level obligations (L0-L3), transition invariant mapping, deterministic failure reasons, and structured JSONL logs with required fields (trace_id, level, invariant_id, check_cmd, result, artifact_ref).","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:01:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5fw.2","title":"Deterministic gate orchestration DAG with fail-fast + resume semantics","description":"Background:
- A closure contract is only useful if there is one deterministic execution order for all gates.

Scope:
- Define and implement canonical gate order: lint -> unit -> conformance -> e2e -> perf -> docs/reports -> release dossier.
- Implement fail-fast semantics with reproducible retries and explicit resume points.

Deliverables:
1) Gate DAG with required inputs/outputs per step.
2) Runner orchestration spec and implementation notes.
3) Operator runbook for partial reruns without invalidating evidence.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Same inputs always produce same gate order and same result.
- Resume behavior is deterministic and auditable.

Rationale:
- Prevents ad-hoc gate sequencing and accidental green builds.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for DAG validity and resume logic.
- E2E tests for fail-fast and resume flows.
- Logs: trace_id, gate_name, prereq_hash, status, duration_ms, resume_token.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.474554818Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:05:32.355931474Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T22:05:32.337574584Z","close_reason":"Implemented release gate DAG artifact + deterministic fail-fast/resume runner + runbook + integration tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["orchestration","release","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1028,"issue_id":"bd-5fw.2","author":"Codex","text":"Starting bd-5fw.2 immediately after closing bd-5fw.1. Plan: define deterministic release gate DAG artifact + implement fail-fast/resume orchestrator script + add harness tests for ordering, fail-fast, and resume-token determinism.","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:02:04Z"},{"id":1029,"issue_id":"bd-5fw.2","author":"Codex","text":"Delivered deterministic release gate DAG + fail-fast/resume runner + runbook + tests.\n\nArtifacts:\n- tests/conformance/release_gate_dag.v1.json\n- scripts/release_dry_run.sh\n- tests/conformance/release_gate_runner_runbook.md\n- crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/release_gate_dag_test.rs\n- scripts/ci.sh (extended gates now include release dry-run orchestration)\n\nVerification:\n- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test release_gate_dag_test -- --nocapture (5 passed)\n- scripts/release_dry_run.sh --mode dry-run (PASS)\n\nFail-fast/resume semantics validated with intentional e2e gate failure and deterministic resume token replay (resume_skip for prior gates; restart at failed gate index).","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:05:32Z"}]}
